title: Top Tech Stocks to Buy Right Now
subtitle: The handful of US and Canadian tech names our editors think are worth owning through the next leg.

children.text: Tech is the broadest sector on the tape, and most lists default to whatever is up and to the right. We don't. This list is filtered for businesses with durable distribution, real cash flow, and a story we'd want to own through a slowdown — not just a melt-up.

picks.thesis: Most defensible distribution moat in software, with Azure AI and Copilot adding a second growth chapter just as the first matures. Capital allocation is best in class.
Services is doing all the lifting on margin and growth, and Apple Intelligence is starting to give the installed base a reason to upgrade again. Boring isn't bad.
Cleaner financial profile post-logistics divestiture, take-rate expansion via Shop Pay and Capital, and improving enterprise GMV mix. Canadian flagship tech you can actually own.
QNX is in 235M+ vehicles and the SDV transition is a multi-year tailwind we don't think the market is paying for. Volatile but asymmetric.

title: Top Financial Stocks to Buy Right Now
subtitle: Banks, insurers, and fintech names that pair quality balance sheets with optionality on the next cycle.

children.text: Financials work when the cycle works. We focus on the names with structural advantages — diversified revenue, fortress capital, dominant deposit franchises — over the ones that just look cheap. Plus one fintech we think is structurally mispriced.

picks.thesis: Best operator in US banking with industry-leading ROTCE and excess capital that gives it counter-cyclical optionality. The Dimon premium is earned.
Anchor Canadian holding. Wealth management is the under-appreciated growth engine, the HSBC Canada acquisition still has synergy room, and the dividend keeps marching higher.
Fintech with a real bank charter, deposit-funded NIM, and a hidden software platform business. Volatile, but the operating fundamentals keep improving.

title: Top Energy Stocks to Buy Right Now
subtitle: Long-life producers and infrastructure names with cash flow that holds through the cycle.

children.text: We don't try to time WTI. We pick names with low break-evens, transparent capital allocation, and dividend track records that survive a $50 oil scare. Both of these names have done it before.

picks.thesis: Lowest break-even in Canadian large-cap energy with the cleanest free-cash-flow allocation framework on the TSX. Pays you to wait through any cycle.
Pipeline backbone with utility-like cash flows, an industry-leading yield, and rate-base growth from the US gas utility additions. The dividend has been raised for 29 consecutive years.
